title: clean-room verifier does not enforce registry-only dependencies or no-wrapping
description:
Evidence:
- README.md:48 and docs/SPECIFICATION.md:243/403 claim only status=verified packages may be imported by clean-room implementations.
- tools/cleanroom_verify.py:58-116 only sets THESEUS_BLOCKED_PACKAGE and runs the invariant function. It does not inspect imports, check imported modules against theseus_registry.json, or reject third-party/original implementation modules.
- tools/lint_cleanroom.py checks ZSDL text only; it does not lint cleanroom/python implementations.
- Verified registry entries include implementations that re-export implementation modules directly, e.g. cleanroom/python/theseus_struct_cr/__init__.py imports _struct and re-exports pack/unpack/Struct; cleanroom/python/theseus_hashlib_cr/__init__.py imports _hashlib/_sha2/_md5; cleanroom/python/theseus_decimal_cr/__init__.py imports _decimal. These are marked status=verified in theseus_registry.json.

Impact:
The central no-wrapping and registry-only dependency guarantee is not enforced. A clean-room package can pass invariants by wrapping lower-level original implementation modules or importing arbitrary dependencies that happen to be installed.

Expected behavior:
Clean-room verification should make dependency policy explicit and enforce it for implementation imports, not only block one exact public module name.
acceptance_criteria:
Define an allowed-import policy for cleanroom/python and cleanroom/node.
Add static or runtime checks that reject imports outside stdlib plus verified Theseus packages, and reject known implementation backdoors for the blocked package.
Add regression tests with a package that imports an unverified module and one that imports a blocked-package implementation module.
Audit existing verified entries and downgrade or fix wrappers that violate the policy.
close_reason: Resolved in code: registry verification now runs cleanroom_verify before promotion; cleanroom_verify enforces Python/Node dependency and no-wrapping policy; node_cleanroom blocks the replaced module; cleanroom metadata is in the schema; synthesis writes are confined to work_dir; harness crash fallback preserves invariant failures; --jobs > 1 is explicitly rejected; Python 3.9 import-time compatibility is restored and CI includes 3.9. Added targeted regression tests and downgraded 32 policy violations to policy_failed with follow-up theseus-x7q.

title: registry verify promotes packages without verification
description:
Evidence:
- README.md and docs/SPECIFICATION.md define status=verified as meaning the clean-room package passed invariants under isolation.
- tools/registry.py:47-53 mark_verified() only loads theseus_registry.json, checks the name exists, sets status to verified, and saves. It never calls tools/cleanroom_verify.py, never checks the spec path, and never validates the implementation path.
- docs/architecture.md:526-528 and docs/cleanroom-spec-format.md:196-199 tell users to run registry.py verify after registration, making this command the gate users rely on.

Impact:
Any registered package can be promoted to verified without passing a single invariant or isolation check. This makes theseus_registry.json unsuitable as the dependency trust root it claims to be.

Expected behavior:
registry.py verify <name> should execute the package's recorded spec through the clean-room verifier and only update status when all invariants pass.
acceptance_criteria:
Add tests showing a broken registered implementation is not promoted.
Make registry.py verify resolve and validate the recorded spec and cleanroom_path.
Run cleanroom_verify for cleanroom backends and fail nonzero without changing status on any failure.
Record verification metadata such as timestamp/pass count or at least make the command output explicit about what was checked.
close_reason: Resolved in code: registry verification now runs cleanroom_verify before promotion; cleanroom_verify enforces Python/Node dependency and no-wrapping policy; node_cleanroom blocks the replaced module; cleanroom metadata is in the schema; synthesis writes are confined to work_dir; harness crash fallback preserves invariant failures; --jobs > 1 is explicitly rejected; Python 3.9 import-time compatibility is restored and CI includes 3.9. Added targeted regression tests and downgraded 32 policy violations to policy_failed with follow-up theseus-x7q.

comments.author: Jordan Hubbard
comments.text: Additional bypass: tools/registry.py register accepts --status with any string, including verified, and writes it directly. Even if verify is fixed, register should not be able to create a verified package without running the gate.

title: Complete s4 CLI synthesis wave
description: Finish the s4 CLI backend synthesis wave by resolving retryable failures, validating terminal state, and pushing the updated wave state and any implementation fixes.
acceptance_criteria: s4 has no pending, failed, partial, or build_failed entries unless an item is terminal infeasible with concrete evidence; reports/synthesis/wave_state.json reflects the final state; relevant checks pass; changes are committed; bd dolt push and git push succeed.

notes: Follow-up correction: after closing, all 17 initially infeasible cr1 entries were directly verified/fixed in the same session; final cr1 state is 390/390 success, 0 failed, 0 infeasible.

title: Complete missing npm synthesis wave

acceptance_criteria: s5 is run or clearly blocked with evidence; wave_state reflects the result; quality checks relevant to changed files pass; changes are committed and pushed.
close_reason: Completed s5 npm wave: 1091/1091 terminal, s5 marked complete; 1066 success and 25 infeasible. Added JavaScript package-name staging and built-in shadowing fixes, validated all specs, and ran focused synthesis tests.

title: synthesis build writes LLM filenames outside the work directory
description:
Evidence:
- PromptBuilder.extract_source_files() accepts arbitrary filename values from <file name="..."> blocks.
- theseus/synthesis/build.py:_write_files writes each file with dest = work_dir / filename, creates dest.parent, and writes content. It does not reject absolute paths, ../ path traversal, symlinks, or paths that resolve outside work_dir.
- SynthesisBuildDriver.build() calls _write_files for Python, C, JavaScript, and Rust synthesis outputs. These filenames are supplied by an LLM response.

Impact:
A malformed or adversarial LLM response can write files outside the intended temp workspace. Even if the current LLM is trusted, this violates the build sandbox boundary and can overwrite repo files or user files when synthesis is run locally.

Expected behavior:
Synthesis file extraction should only materialize relative paths contained under the per-attempt work_dir.
acceptance_criteria:
Add unit tests for absolute paths and ../ traversal in source file names.
Normalize and resolve destinations before writing, and reject anything outside work_dir.
Reject or ignore symlink traversal if parent directories already exist.
Return a build failure with a clear diagnostic instead of writing unsafe paths.
close_reason: Resolved in code: registry verification now runs cleanroom_verify before promotion; cleanroom_verify enforces Python/Node dependency and no-wrapping policy; node_cleanroom blocks the replaced module; cleanroom metadata is in the schema; synthesis writes are confined to work_dir; harness crash fallback preserves invariant failures; --jobs > 1 is explicitly rejected; Python 3.9 import-time compatibility is restored and CI includes 3.9. Added targeted regression tests and downgraded 32 policy violations to policy_failed with follow-up theseus-x7q.

title: Z-spec schema rejects cleanroom compiler metadata when jsonschema is installed
description:
Evidence:
- tools/zsdl_compile.py:274-286 emits top-level backend_lang and cleanroom_path for cleanroom backends, and tools/zsdl_compile.py:288-290 emits top-level blocks when present.
- tests/test_cleanroom_backend.py asserts backend_lang and cleanroom_path are present in compiled cleanroom specs.
- zspecs/schema/behavioral-spec.schema.json has additionalProperties: false at the top level and does not define backend_lang, cleanroom_path, or blocks.
- tools/validate_zspec.py uses jsonschema validation when the jsonschema package is installed, otherwise it falls back to a permissive stdlib check.

Impact:
Validation behavior depends on whether jsonschema happens to be installed. In an environment with jsonschema, valid cleanroom specs produced by the compiler can fail schema validation solely because the schema is stale. In the current local fallback path, that mismatch is hidden.

Expected behavior:
The JSON schema and validator should agree with the compiler output for cleanroom specs.
acceptance_criteria:
Update zspecs/schema/behavioral-spec.schema.json to define backend_lang, cleanroom_path, and blocks or move them under an existing allowed metadata object.
Add tests that exercise validate_zspec with the jsonschema path for a compiled python_cleanroom and node_cleanroom spec.
Ensure validation results do not differ between jsonschema and stdlib fallback for these fields.
close_reason: Resolved in code: registry verification now runs cleanroom_verify before promotion; cleanroom_verify enforces Python/Node dependency and no-wrapping policy; node_cleanroom blocks the replaced module; cleanroom metadata is in the schema; synthesis writes are confined to work_dir; harness crash fallback preserves invariant failures; --jobs > 1 is explicitly rejected; Python 3.9 import-time compatibility is restored and CI includes 3.9. Added targeted regression tests and downgraded 32 policy violations to policy_failed with follow-up theseus-x7q.

title: node_cleanroom isolation does not block the replaced module
description:
Evidence:
- zspecs/theseus_path_node.zspec.zsdl declares blocks: path and its provenance notes explicitly say not to require('path').
- docs/cleanroom-spec-format.md:15 and docs/architecture.md:482 claim the original module is actively blocked during verification.
- tools/cleanroom_verify.py:_verify_node builds a Node script that simply require()s the cleanroom index.js and calls the exported function. It does not read spec["blocks"], does not patch Module._load, and does not otherwise prevent require("path") or require of any npm package.

Impact:
A node_cleanroom implementation can require the original Node module or an npm dependency and still be reported as passing. The one committed Node clean-room backend has no isolation mechanism equivalent to cleanroom/python/sitecustomize.py.

Expected behavior:
Node clean-room verification should block require/import of the blocked module and enforce the no-npm-dependency rule before declaring success.
acceptance_criteria:
Add a node_cleanroom test fixture whose implementation requires the blocked module and confirm verification fails.
Implement a Node isolation prelude that blocks spec["blocks"] for CommonJS require and dynamic import where feasible.
Add checks for unexpected npm package imports or document/enforce an allowed list.
Make failure messages identify the offending require/import.
close_reason: Resolved in code: registry verification now runs cleanroom_verify before promotion; cleanroom_verify enforces Python/Node dependency and no-wrapping policy; node_cleanroom blocks the replaced module; cleanroom metadata is in the schema; synthesis writes are confined to work_dir; harness crash fallback preserves invariant failures; --jobs > 1 is explicitly rejected; Python 3.9 import-time compatibility is restored and CI includes 3.9. Added targeted regression tests and downgraded 32 policy violations to policy_failed with follow-up theseus-x7q.

Local tests and docs build pass.

title: Revisit cr1 infeasible clean-room targets
description: cr1 is complete as a wave, but 17 clean-room targets remain terminal infeasible and should be retried or handled manually in a follow-up pass. Infeasible entries: theseus_ast_cr, theseus_compression_zlib_cr, theseus_dataclasses_cr3, theseus_email_base64mime_cr, theseus_linecache_cr, theseus_mimetypes_cr, theseus_ntpath_cr, theseus_pwd_cr, theseus_re_cr3, theseus_re_cr4, theseus_re_cr5, theseus_struct_cr, theseus_this_cr, theseus_tomllib_cr, theseus_urllib_parse_cr, theseus_weakref_extra_cr, theseus_zlib_cr3.
acceptance_criteria: Each listed cr1 package is either verified successfully with tools/cleanroom_verify.py and wave_state updated, or left infeasible with a concrete non-LLM-availability reason documented in the issue or wave state.

title: Rewrite policy-failed clean-room packages without blocked imports
description:
The clean-room verifier now rejects imports of blocked public modules, blocked implementation backdoors, unverified Theseus packages, and third-party dependencies. After applying that policy, 32 existing registry entries were downgraded from verified to policy_failed in theseus_registry.json.

These packages should be rewritten as true clean-room implementations, or their specs/registry entries should be retired if the package cannot be implemented without wrapping the blocked module.

Acceptance criteria:
- For each policy_failed package, remove imports of the blocked package or implementation backdoor.
- Run tools/cleanroom_verify.py on the recorded spec and ensure all invariants pass.
- Promote through tools/registry.py verify, not by editing status directly.
- Keep theseus_registry.json counts/docs consistent after packages are restored.
acceptance_criteria: All policy_failed entries in theseus_registry.json are either verified via tools/registry.py verify or explicitly retired with a documented reason.
close_reason: Resolved: rewrote all 32 policy_failed clean-room implementations so they no longer import blocked public modules or private implementation backdoors. Verified each with tools/cleanroom_verify.py, promoted all through tools/registry.py verify, cleared stale policy_error metadata, restored registry/docs to 392 verified packages, and added verifier/registry regression tests.

title: Python version policy is inconsistent across docs, Makefile, CI, and tools
description:
Evidence:
- AGENTS.md and docs/cleanroom-spec-format.md state Python 3.9 compatibility is required. The Makefile only checks sys.version_info >= (3, 9).
- .github/workflows/ci.yml explicitly drops Python 3.9 and comments that the codebase uses PEP 604 union syntax introduced in 3.10.
- Several executable tools lack `from __future__ import annotations` while using PEP 604 annotations, including tools/verify_behavior.py, tools/verify_in_docker.py, tools/search_specs.py, tools/provenance_report.py, and tools/registry.py.
- In this shell, the repo-default `python3` resolves to /usr/bin/python3 3.9.6; `python3 tools/verify_behavior.py --help` and `python3 tools/registry.py check theseus_json` fail at import time with TypeError for `str | None`.

Impact:
The advertised default workflow can fail before tests or verification start on systems where python3 is 3.9. The project simultaneously claims 3.9 support and excludes it in CI, making contributors chase the wrong compatibility target.

Expected behavior:
The repo should either actually support Python 3.9 or consistently require Python 3.10+ everywhere.
acceptance_criteria:
Decide and document the supported Python floor in README, AGENTS.md, docs, Makefile, and CI.
If 3.9 remains supported, add future annotations or rewrite PEP 604 annotations in all importable tools and restore CI coverage.
If 3.10+ is required, update Makefile checks and quick-start docs so python3.9 fails with a clear version error before imports.
Add a lightweight test or CI check that catches incompatible syntax for the declared floor.
close_reason: Resolved in code: registry verification now runs cleanroom_verify before promotion; cleanroom_verify enforces Python/Node dependency and no-wrapping policy; node_cleanroom blocks the replaced module; cleanroom metadata is in the schema; synthesis writes are confined to work_dir; harness crash fallback preserves invariant failures; --jobs > 1 is explicitly rejected; Python 3.9 import-time compatibility is restored and CI includes 3.9. Added targeted regression tests and downgraded 32 policy violations to policy_failed with follow-up theseus-x7q.

title: synthesize_waves exposes --jobs but runs specs serially
description:
Evidence:
- tools/synthesize_waves.py registers --jobs with help text "Parallel workers" and passes jobs into cmd_run_wave().
- cmd_run_wave() accepts the jobs parameter but never reads it; it loops over pending specs with `for i, meta in enumerate(pending, 1)` and processes one spec at a time.
- tools/run_pipeline.py implements actual ThreadPoolExecutor behavior for its own --jobs flag, so the expected pattern exists elsewhere in the repo.

Impact:
Users can request parallel wave synthesis and get silently serial execution. For large clean-room waves, this makes the CLI misleading and can waste substantial time.

Expected behavior:
Either implement bounded parallelism for wave synthesis or remove/disable the --jobs flag with an explicit message.
acceptance_criteria:
Add a test or integration-level check that --jobs > 1 affects scheduling or is rejected.
Implement parallel execution with safe per-spec state updates, or remove --jobs from synthesize_waves.
Document any concurrency limitations around LLM rate limits and wave_state writes.
close_reason: Resolved in code: registry verification now runs cleanroom_verify before promotion; cleanroom_verify enforces Python/Node dependency and no-wrapping policy; node_cleanroom blocks the replaced module; cleanroom metadata is in the schema; synthesis writes are confined to work_dir; harness crash fallback preserves invariant failures; --jobs > 1 is explicitly rejected; Python 3.9 import-time compatibility is restored and CI includes 3.9. Added targeted regression tests and downgraded 32 policy violations to policy_failed with follow-up theseus-x7q.

title: synthesis runner loses harness crash details and can report zero-result runs as infeasible
description:
Evidence:
- theseus/synthesis/runner.py:_invoke_verify_harness writes --json-out, runs tools/verify_behavior.py, and if the JSON file is absent/empty it tries to return a failed result list.
- That fallback currently iterates `for inv in []`, so it always returns an empty list and drops the harness stderr/exit code.
- SynthesisRunner.run() then sees pass_count=0 and fail_count=0, sets status to infeasible, and loses which invariant or harness condition failed.

Impact:
If verify_behavior.py crashes, times out before JSON output, or fails to import under the selected Python, the synthesis loop receives no actionable failure details. This can incorrectly classify infrastructure failures as synthesis infeasibility and prevents revision prompts from containing the actual error.

Expected behavior:
A harness crash should produce explicit failed invariant records or a distinct runner error status with the captured stderr.
acceptance_criteria:
Pass the spec/invariant list into the harness fallback path or return a structured harness_error result.
Add a test where subprocess.run returns nonzero and no JSON output, asserting failures are not an empty list.
Preserve stderr/stdout in SynthesisResult notes or failed_invariant_details.
Ensure wave_state records distinguish harness failure from LLM infeasibility.
close_reason: Resolved in code: registry verification now runs cleanroom_verify before promotion; cleanroom_verify enforces Python/Node dependency and no-wrapping policy; node_cleanroom blocks the replaced module; cleanroom metadata is in the schema; synthesis writes are confined to work_dir; harness crash fallback preserves invariant failures; --jobs > 1 is explicitly rejected; Python 3.9 import-time compatibility is restored and CI includes 3.9. Added targeted regression tests and downgraded 32 policy violations to policy_failed with follow-up theseus-x7q.

title: Re-run OSS Portal dependency checklist
description: After the dependency evidence changes are pushed, re-run the external OSS Portal analysis/checklist for Theseus and confirm the dependency-extraction score is at least 8/10. No local oss-portal CLI was available in the repository environment during the implementation session.
acceptance_criteria: OSS Portal dependency-extraction checklist is rerun against the pushed repository; resulting score and remaining findings are recorded in Beads.
notes: 2026-05-06: Confirmed oss-portal exposes the API contract through FastAPI OpenAPI at /api/openapi.json and route models in backend/app/schemas. Ran a local TestClient contract flow against Theseus HEAD: POST /api/auth/login, POST /api/projects, POST /api/projects/{id}/source-upload with git archive of Theseus HEAD, POST /api/projects/{id}/submit, GET /api/analysis-runs/{run_id}, GET /api/projects/{id}/skill-checklist, GET /api/projects/{id}/checklist-coaching. Result: analysis_status=completed, aggregate_score=84, dependency-extraction status=succeeded, score=9, passed=true, findings=[]. Caveat: local oss-portal .env has no LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY, so the run used the mock LLM provider; a production/real-provider portal rerun is still needed for final checklist confirmation.

title: Make test should skip or provision optional behavior dependencies
description: During s4 completion on 2026-05-06, PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH make test compiled and validated specs, then failed in the full pytest suite because the local Python 3.14 environment lacks many optional behavior-test libraries such as attr, filelock, fsspec, markdown, markupsafe, more_itertools, packaging, Pillow, protobuf, psutil, pyparsing, setuptools, stevedore, tornado, traitlets, wrapt, and zope.interface. The final summary was 74 failed, 4576 passed, 60 skipped, 736 errors. These tests should either declare/provision their dev dependencies or skip cleanly when optional third-party packages are absent.
acceptance_criteria: make test has a documented/provisioned dependency setup that passes in a supported environment, or optional third-party behavior suites use pytest.importorskip/shutil.which-style guards so missing optional packages do not produce errors.
close_reason: make test skips unavailable optional behavior-test dependencies, version fallback handles simple multi-clause constraints without packaging, and full make test passes locally.
